name: Strait of Malacca
climate: Tropical equatorial; hot and humid year-round; heavy monsoon rainfall; frequent thunderstorms

neighbors.name: ASEAN
neighbors.relevance: Littoral states Malaysia, Singapore, and Indonesia are ASEAN members; strait governance is an ASEAN concern

neighbors.name: South China Sea
neighbors.relevance: Eastern exit of Malacca feeds directly into SCS shipping lanes

neighbors.name: Indo-Pacific
neighbors.relevance: Malacca is the most critical chokepoint in the Indo-Pacific maritime domain

neighbors.name: Pearl River Delta
neighbors.relevance: PRD-bound container and energy traffic constitutes a major share of Malacca throughput

neighbors.name: Persian Gulf
neighbors.relevance: Gulf oil tankers transiting to East Asia must pass through Malacca or alternative straits

links.description: Malacca is the western chokepoint of SCS shipping; ~25% of global seaborne trade transits both
Three ASEAN members govern the strait; ASEAN maritime cooperation framework applies
Gulf crude oil and LNG bound for East Asia transit Malacca as the shortest route
Hormuz-to-Malacca is the primary energy transit corridor feeding Northeast Asian economies
Malacca handles the bulk of raw material imports destined for PRD manufacturing
Malacca links the Indian Ocean to the Pacific; its security underpins Indo-Pacific trade
Maritime Silk Road transits Malacca; China invests in alternative routes to reduce dependency
